title: Crosswalks CDE ⇄ mCODE/FHIR, OMOP CDM, GA4GH Phenopackets
context: The project's poolability proof — that a registry built from the CDE templates is automatically compatible with the major open-oncology ecosystems — depends on deterministic crosswalk mappings from the rare-cancer CDE set to mCODE/FHIR, OMOP CDM, and GA4GH Phenopackets. These crosswalks must be machine-readable and proven by a synthetic round-trip conformance test. Every crosswalk row must carry provenance, and lossy mappings must be documented rather than silently dropped. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-vocab-001. Standards reviewer sign-off required.
objective: Produce machine-readable crosswalk tables in `crosswalks/` mapping the CDE set bi-directionally to: (1) mCODE/HL7 FHIR profiles, (2) OMOP CDM (open vocabularies only), and (3) GA4GH Phenopackets. Each crosswalk row must carry provenance and document any lossy or approximate mappings. Prove correctness with a synthetic record that round-trips through all three targets and validates against each.
acceptanceCriteria: Crosswalk tables covering CDE ⇄ mCODE/FHIR, CDE ⇄ OMOP CDM, and CDE ⇄ GA4GH Phenopackets are committed in `crosswalks/` as machine-readable files.
Every crosswalk row carries provenance (source CDE, target system element, mapping rationale, retrieval date, licence note) per the prov-001 model.
Lossy or approximate mappings are explicitly documented with a `lossNote` or equivalent field; no mapping is silently dropped.
A synthetic record expressed in the CDE set round-trips to mCODE FHIR, OMOP CDM, and Phenopackets and validates against each target schema.
OMOP CDM crosswalk uses only open member vocabularies (no SNOMED or CPT-licensed vocabs); CI licence linter green.
Standards reviewer sign-off recorded; `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green.
output: A `crosswalks/` directory with machine-readable CDE ⇄ mCODE/FHIR, CDE ⇄ OMOP CDM, and CDE ⇄ Phenopackets crosswalk tables plus a round-trip conformance test on synthetic data, committed via PR with standards reviewer sign-off.
